name: OverDrift Festival
review:
Another cashgrab game. Honestly I want to "Not Recommend" because of the grind level of this, but hey! I had made up a solution for that. So it wasn't that bad.

Plus this definitely isnt a game where you play for 10 hours and get pro, every car's cash system is unique, which can be bypassed by making points on a 100% car and cashing on the lower ones. Atleast that option is there. 

MAKE FRIENDS. Its an Online-Only game... so what are waiting for? There's no fun in drifting alone. Tandeming is peak tbh, I spent over 6 months just trying to get a tune right (and no I didnt pick anyone else's tunes I make my own). This game REQUIRES you to hook on it. 
Neverthless I have awesome buddies in the game that keep pulling me here and prevent me from hitting uninstall. So there goes another 500 hours ig.......

name: OverDrift Festival
review: When the game was small and unpopular, it was great. Now that it's famous, developers saw the opportunity to put as much microtransactions in the game as possible. 50% of the game is locked behing microtransactions actually. Initally you were able to obtain every car simply just by grinding. Now you can buy like 8 free cars and the rest you have to buy with DLCs and THEN grind for them and buy them with the in-game currency. If the developers don't A) lower the prices of the DLCs, because they are ridiculously expensive (you can get a whole game for the price of some of them) or B) remove them entirely and let players enjoy playing a game they paid not so small money for, players are definitelly going to quit playing it, like I am now. Maybe if they didn't give away tens of steam codes for free, they wouldn't need to scrub as much money off of the players as possible.

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
I Have 364 hours in the game, and I got it very close to release. The game was WAY better then. Now it's a micro transaction simulator. This became a perfect example of was made by somebody that had good faith, but got the taste of money and wanted more. I really was hopeful for this game on release. Now I hope it dies, and hopefully something else will replace it.

Most cars and modifications to those cars are behind a paywall. Even then liveries are capped at 99 layers, and you have no actual customization to the body, like want a body kit? That's a whole different car in game that you have to pay for with actual money. 

You have to map all controls manually, and even then it breaks and you can no longer use a controller or wheel randomly. I was going to use the controller I always used on this game and it simply won't map correctly anymore. (This was my final straw coming back to the game at the time of writing this.) 

The handling of the cars is honestly not horrible, but it's not far off. This game is more arcade then sim, and the cars are a nightmare to drive with anything less then 50% of the max upgrades that you have to unlock by drifting and earning money on EACH car you unlock. Or pay for DLCs and get them all at 50% and still have to get them to 100%. There is a lot of grinding to get even one 100% car I have over 20 each costing around 100-200k credits in-game, and I have 2million credits in a car that I can't use on any other cars as that cash is locked to that car....

The community is EXTREMELY toxic. Even making a few people I know have IRL problems.
Many "clans" gate keeps others from playing by ramming, and bulling them online. Keep in mind this is a fully online game. You can create servers with a player cap or password to play by your self or friends you already know, but then you can't actually play the game as it is made to.

🔎 Long Version Checklist ➜ [u][url=https://steamcommunity.com/groups/expectationchecklist/discussions/1/4763207146661766064/]Reveal[/url][/u]

[h1] 🕘          IMPRESSION:[/h1]
* Year 2022: Game released 
* 15 min later: Started playtest on Demo, sadly it is not what I expected
* Year 2024: A well-edited video review pulled me back in
* 2 hours earlier: Cannot find an informative review but "very positive" rating on Steam?
* 2 hours later: Uncover the facts behind the beautiful rating

[h1]  🔊         TESTER MUST KNOW:[/h1]
* We can create a server with custom setting
* Click and wait 5 sec to teleport in the map
* Damage Penalty = Losing drift ability 
* Free roam to earn style points & convert into cash
* Enter cash zone to earn style points money
* Earned money is tied to each car, cannot spend on another car
* Disqualify event when player gone too far away from the circuit
* Lack of community livery to download, limited to 1 livery for only specific car
* Downloaded free demo will not able to join server anymore

[h1] 💎 SAME GENRE COMPARISON:[/h1]
* No race menu, must fast travel to each location to start a game
* No track wall, no AI bot, no ghost car
* Complete multiple circuit mode to unlock more cars
* Completed circuit track will not mark as completed
* Car collision with online players when drifting
* Deep customization option on tuning
* Many visual customization moved to DLC
* All the beautiful cars moved to DLC
* List of repetitive challenges for Daily Task & Weekly Task
* No ping display in the server list

[h1]  ⚠️        RUMOR CHECKING:[/h1]
* It is a fun game to play ➜ Happen only if you love free roam with public players
* Huge desync between players & poor optimized ➜ Disagree
* UI is terrible ➜ Because there are too many buttons on the screen at once
* Car become undriftable when drifting is the only way to earn money ➜ True, when the money is tied to each car, and damaged car require money to repair. This idea makes the car unplayable.

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
A really cool drifting game just for the fun of it.
The community seems to be very friendly. You either encounter people who don't communicate at all or do it in a friendly way. I have yet to encounter anyone who is rude or toxic.

The driving physics feel great. Or should I say drifting physics, since this is what the game is all about?

The customization is nice! You don't have to stick to premade liveries, you can create your very own one ingame.
Lots of decals, wheels, tires, exhausts, etc. to choose from. Sadly no wings though. I'd love to put the iconic Evo2 wing on my 190E.

Money is earned by drifting, either in events or just in freeride.

Why so many negative reviews? 
While the people do have a point about the dlc stuff and having some customization like neon and tire smoke behind a paywall, they are exaggerating badly by saying "almost all cars are behind paywall". That's not true. You can unlock many cars with just the base game. I bought the "overpass" and the car pack that included my dream car. That's all I'll ever need in the game.

The graphics are quite okay, definitely better than comparable drift games though.

The sound design isn't too great. The car sounds are low quality and the music... ugh... It's a matter of your own personal music taste, but I'm grateful for the ability to turn the music volume to 0%. All in game music is some kind of techno dubsteb gangsterrap combined.

It plays very well with a steering wheel and uses 900°, so no worries, no only 180° ratio like in other games.

What definitely needs to be added in my opinion is an option to make the camera automatically look in the direction your drifting in cokpit view. I play only in cockpit view and tandem drifting is basically a gamble because I can't see the other car or even the track. 
Or maybe even a VR option. That would be ideal for me.

All in all probably one of the best dedicated drifting games around!

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
the game started off as a second home with plenty of help for beginners and a great way to meet new people, but seemingly over a month or so, the community has begun to eat themselves alive over an apparent club war. people disrespecting people for their affiliation to a team, or the color of their car. 

This is no longer a safe place to come to for even thick skin members because at the core of the issue, this was meant to be a drifting game for people to enjoy TOGETHER. and even as fun as the driving in the game is, it's over shadowed by the sheer toxic behavior slowly growing in this very small community. 

you will see everybody you drift with again, the servers are not larger or random, and this can be good or bad. it used to be fun seeing everybody day after day and getting close with people.

my only regret now is i cant get a refund after 700 hours.

Update: your community has bred nothing but hatred, and arrogance. somebody tell me when it shares the Fate of furidashi

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
In short: Mid game with too much grind, easy loss of your main way to get cash, screwing yourself over with a car, and people can force you to lose your cash gain.

In long: $10 game with $92 in car DLC packs. $42 of which are for 'exclusive' DLC car pack in 4 different packs. All of these packs have the cars automatically 50% upgraded to avoid the slow grind of the early-game of each car.

Yeah, each car earns money exclusively for itself. And thats done, efficently, by having a long string of drifts. Without having a hard impact on something and sitting still for a couple of seconds. Problem that has arisen recently: Some people in the community at large are dickheads that'll slam into you trying to pass. Would've had 100k points to bank? Buh-Bye, wasted time and you still keep the car damage caused by all of it.
   Thats another thing. Your car's tires, engine, and steering slowly take damage as you slide it at the speeds you should. Loss of power and too much loss of traction? Just get money. But now you can't get that money quick now!

Want to be a cheap player and only buy the game? Good luck, you have to grind a lot to get regular levels, ignoring the rim unlocks, and HOPE you get a car model you want. And thats on top of gaining less drift points because you don't have the premium game pass for an extra $10. Which has a 2nd level system with pre-liveried cars with 50% of upgrades done already.

TLDR: Community policing itself is waning, confirmed by someone who's tried to get action taken. Game's too much grind. And the DLCs are a cashgrab now.
Newbie hostile to a massive degree.

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
By far one of the best drift focused games. Very easy going and simple to play, launch the game, pick a server, and jump right into drifting with other players.

Very good car list, satisfies a lot of different styles and cultures, however, the car unlock system really drags this game out and is super unnecessary, the idea of unlocking one car per level is good, however the RNG aspect makes it a complete chore to unlock the cars you want, as of writing this review at 31 hours i still haven't unlocked any of the great cars the game has to offer that i personally want.

The car upgrade system is very straight forward and straight to the point, and the menus are easy to navigate. Each upgrade works like its supposed to with noticeable difference and the upgrade progression is smooth and not too grindy. Car tuning is precise and allows you fine tune almost every aspect of the car with also noticeable difference.

The game looks and feels amazing, compared to other drift games this one is by far one of the best looking and most polished. The way the cars react to steering and weight transfer also makes the flow of drifting very satisfying and stable. The maps are well laid out and offer a variety of different styles of drift courses/tracks which are all both fun and competitive in their own aspect.

The car customisation feels a little bit lacking as there is no way to physically change the look of the car with body kits, bumpers, and wings, as the only way is to buy a DLC version of the car which also has a set style and look. Something that stood out to me was the interior customisation, being able to change interior colours and materials unlocks a lot of room for creativity and exclusivity. A livery system is included as well as a way to share them with other players which is a big aspect of most racing game communities.

The controls are very simple to set up and do not take long at all, they're also extremely easy to pick up and get used to if you're familiar with other racing games. The Keyboard experience is actually enjoyable and easy to master, enabling steering assist makes the movements a lot smoother and less jagged without altering the difficulty or gameplay at all really. Playing on controller is also very light and not too snappy and difficult, without much setup controller is already extremely smooth and easy to handle. As for wheel gameplay there is nothing to complain about there either, very smooth with multiple settings to play with to make the controls just how you like them.

Physics: 9/10 predictable and smooth, somewhat arcadey but utilises real world drifting mechanics
Controls: 10/10 very easy to set up and master for all supported devices
Car list: 8/10 fairly sized car roster with lots of variety
Progression: 3/10 car unlock system needs a complete revamp however upgrade progression is perfect
Customisation: 6/10 great wheel options, interior, and paint/livery customisation, just missing exterior body customisation.
Graphics: 8/10 car models are very polished and all visual aspects look stunning and are very well put together
Upgrades/Tuning: 10/10 very straight forward with lots of tuning parameters to fine tune to your liking

Overall: a strong 8.5/10, just a very relaxing yet competitive game to quickly boot up and dive straight into.

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
Honestly, this game used to be so fun just to kick back and drive. There used to be so many cars you can unlock WITHOUT having to buy the dlc(you can get just the base car without a livery). Now it's just a grind fest. They took away the 25 free gifts around the map, the car unlock tree, and locked the new car models behind the DLC paywall instead of just releasing it for everyone. Imagine buying a dlc pack thats priced higher than the base game.

 If you just want to drive, its cool. Customization is very very lacking in that department. Only cool things you can do is change wheels, muffler, and livery editor. 
Want to change the body kit? Can't. 
Want just a basic single exit or dual exit muffler? only one is a classic single exit that isnt one of the stupid glorified motorcycle exhaust that stick up in the air 3 feet. 
Did I mention that theres like 20 of those out of like 25 exhaust options? 
Want to tune your car? you can, but it literally makes no difference on how the car drives. 

What kind of stuff can you do besides driving around the map blind?
Battles are cool to do, with clipping points you can target for more points.
Theres a big F on the map for like a freestyle type of event?
and maybe one more style of event that no one does because it doesnt make any sense at all?
No matter if your doing an event or not, the completion rewards is EXTREMELY small, so its not even worth doing them.
You want money? just keep driving all day and you'll have enough to upgrade one or two parts.

Every single update they do either adds another DLC car pack which is just copy paste physics on every car, with sometimes a new model, and maybe a new preset livery. Or they just completely remove a key feature that made the game good. OR maybe they add a bunch of features that NO ONE USES  AND IS ALWAYS BROKEN LIKE THE OVERPASS(season pass???).

Conclusion: if you want to just have fun drifting with some friends or alone, its fun. If you were expecting a game that had tons of customization and would let you do whatever you want to your car to make it your own, this is NOT that game.

review:
Definitely the best drifting game I've played in a while. 
-Selection of cars is amazing
-Customisation is amazing
-Physics feel amazing
-Game looks amazing. 

The only two things i'd change with the game right now are :
-The servers have pretty bad lag compensation (for me being an NA player, it has a couple seconds of desync.)
-The handbrake needs a bit more tweaking as it feels pretty weak right now.

The best part of the game surely has to be the Tandem lead/chase feature. For this price i say you have no reason to not buy it. The livery editor is also pretty nice to work with.  

I give it a solid 8.5/10 as of right now, could easily be a 10 with more updates.

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
Its good, however.

Its not.

I bought this game looking for a more realistic looking game over Car x Drift racing, Save yourself some time, buy Car X, Mod it with Kino.

While this game has a much better visual package and physics, everything down to car customization, performance upgrades, and more are expensive with the in came currency, which takes ALOT of grinding.

There is a car damage mechanic, which makes sense, an actual drift car takes alot of abuse. However, with how slowly you earn funds, (20k drift points equating to about 5k in game cash) They should either slow down the degradation of your car, or make it cheaper to repair, buff the amount of cash received, etc.

ALOT. And I mean ALOT of content is locked behind a paywall. And with the deluxe edition being $50 full price, I have to say, it really just isnt worth it, people are drawn to games like these with the want to customize their cars, and with how this game works. You feel like you are literally working another job trying to put a singular vinyl on your car or change the paint color.

It is WAAAYYYY to grindy.

Next thing is the actual mechanics for drifting.

Imagine having a combo that would equate to about 35,000 in game dollars, You're in the zone, feeling like the ghost of akina himself, you're coming around the corner, you drift tap your rear end(Not crash) and you loose your entire combo.

Yeah. It happened to me, Needless to say, It seriously put me off of this game.

As many others said, the game has great potential. But the devs really really need to think about things. Because in its current state, I cant recommend this game to anyone.

review:
Honestly a really fun game, i love the fact its open world and u can drive/teleport anywhere on the map within seconds, the graphics is great, and the car packs offers a lot of content for what u pay for, although im usualy against pay to win type of packs. Also a huge bonus that the game gets updated quite often and the developers genuinely seem to care to update the game and listen to the community wich is great!

However there is a few major cons about this game i'd like to see fixed/improved in the future. For starters there is massive server desync when u try to tandem with other people, Second of all no body modifications? and third of all when ur making liveries there should be an autosave/manual save feature on unfinished projects so that u don't have to do it all in 1 go. Also no way of sharing liveries with friends wich is sad. And also for cinematic/photo settings it lacks on some features compared to games like CarX and Forza Horizon 4/5.
Also there is no mods for the game wich is kinda sad, that's what makes games like CarX and Assetto Corsa so great so would love to see that in the future.

It will be probably take a while before this game can be compared with something like CarX/Assetto Corsa but im hoping it'll be better with time as it is extremly fun to play

Other than that i can definately recommend picking up the game.

Overall rating of the game : 8/10

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
Super fucking fun game. I played for 10 minutes and bought the full game right away to support it. The way this game has improved so much and developers know what players need makes me happy to find this game. Reviewing this at 3.6 hours playtime but watch me rack up hundreds of hours after this!

What they did right: 
1. Physics: 10/10 
- best feature compared to other games.
- grabbed a wheel and started drifting. I for the love of god find it so hard to drift on other games, so that says something.

2. Graphics: 8/10 
- very optimized (high fps, not that we need it for this type of game but it’s nice.
- ultrawide monitor support (I use 32:9 monitor)
- textures, shadows, etc feels perfect (not too highly demanding nor too cheap) so many players can hop on.

3. Content: 10/10
- they. got. cities. and working billboards. genius.
- city feels so much alive without having to put AI characters that may ruin the drifting experience. 
- map is so huge! variety of drift tracks with different drift styles in different environments.
- maps also inspired by Japanese and American culture, it actually feels fitting, so that’s nice.

Availability/compatibility: 10/10
- this game just released a demo version which is essentially a free version of the game. it does not restrict any content, except for progress-saving and creating your own server. this is probably the most genius way to reach out to new players. good job dev, this is what pulled me to the game in the first place.
- game is not that big for storage which is so surprising! 
- wheel support works like a charm, easy setup. I use CSL DD that’s usually not supported on other games.
- also works perfectly on Steam Deck so that’s nice.

My advice for those who just found this game and going through reviews first: just download the demo and give it a try. if you like it, support the devs! 

Developers, here are some suggestions to improve the game:
the menu and UI is so lacking in terms of design. it feels very cheaply made and confusing especially in the tuning parts. I think it does not reflect the amazing efforts that you guys put in the gameplay. car selection and tuning is a huge part of this type of game and I think you guys can improve it at least by cleaning up the menu. Maps menu and teleporting was an amazing feature but again it feels a bit cheap in terms of the look. the UI for gaining points, cash, etc could pop out more! right now they look like one of those mobile games with so many ads.
- the highway areas and outside cities, it feels a bit ‘dead’ in a way. the billboards worked fine for the city, but something could be done for the other areas. 

Other than that, it is an amazing game.

Maps: 9/10

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
T500RS review:

Mixed feelings for this one. Its a fun game with great gameplay. But its very arcady and the force feedback is not that good. Let me explain:

The autosteering is off, its hard to explain but ill try. The car straightens too much because the autosteering is countersteering too much so you constantly have to "steer" into the corner to stay drifting. (Im not talking about assists or anything like that, if you know you know...) And another thing is that it feels like the autosteer starts to slow during a transition and then the turning speed picks up at the end. (Ive got around 2000h in sim driving, my FFB settings are not the issue).

The rear is not really controlled with throttle but rather using the steeringwheel and the weight transfer is almost nonexistent.
Its hard to explain but its very off from RL. This is not a realistic sim but its playable but it takes some getting used to, car upgrades and some extreme tuning makes it OK. 

Even tho it has issues with FFB i really like this game, if you are thinking of buying it just be aware of this if you are playing with a wheel. 

What makes me like this game is the community and the gameplay. People are really nice and helpful. The maps are amazing. If they just tune the force feedback a little bit this will be a really good game!

As of right now its playable and fun and if they improve the FFB i will update this review.

Refunded for now. Game seems to be an asset flip. This dev has made about 3 other games that I know of, Peak Angle, Furadashi or whatever and RDS. To add: The fact paid DLC is available for early access with the game having a 30.00 price tag, seems a bit fishy. 
Servers are timed which is odd.

** Update
Its getting better, but still don't quite recommend it. If there is a deep sale, then go for it. I ended up buying it again with a DLC included because Car X street got delayed again. 
Lobbies are a bit dead most of the time with maybe 1 with a decent amount of people. Sometimes there are two lobbies going so the game is growing though at a slower pace. 
Game rarely crashes, but it does on occasion.

Seems to have died and probably will be abandoned. Its too late for me to refund, but you can still save yourself!

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
Absolute crap. That is my simplified overall view of this game. It is most definitely not worth the money they are asking for, especially for the additional DLC. For reference, I use a controller with my driving games. I do not use a steering wheel, so I cannot comment on how it is when using one in this game.

Pros:
- Well, it looks good for the most part. But, looking good can only get you so far.
- That's pretty much it.

Cons:
- There are some obvious spelling issues.
- There is no mouse support. So, when it first starts up, you're going to instinctively move the mouse and see about clicking on the whatever servers are available at the time to find that doesn't work. You have to use the arrow keys on your keyboard. This is the first game I've ever encountered that didn't have basic keyboard AND MOUSE support. Why the developers thought this was a smart idea is beyond me, as it makes absolutely no sense whatsoever.
- For me, the game started up in a small window; I'd say it was about 800 x 600. Which made seeing it on my 65" 4k TV difficult to figure out what I was supposed to do to start an actual game (which only showed 3 "sessions/servers" to choose from). I had to hit Ctrl + Enter to force it into full-screen just so I could continue.
- No "main menu" from the start to setup the game to your liking. No, common sense and basic human intelligence were not considered when it came to making this game. You do not gain access to the menu system until after you've picked a session to join.
- The overall main menu is a clusterf**k and looks sloppy/busy.
- The Graphics Settings menu doesn't actually do a proper full-screen ability. You can choose different resolutions to try and make it fill the entire screen, but it's not an actual full-screen. It's more like full windowed mode.
- I could not enable V-sync. It was an option, but it was an option that you could not enable.
- Control mapping is defaulted to the keyboard. This is almost always a sign that the people responsible for this genuinely do not know what they're doing when it comes to making a game. A DRIVING game. With keyboard mapping as default. A DRIVING. GAME. When the usual thing you'd use is a controller or a steering wheel. And, the control mapping menu doesn't even have the ability to give a selection of whether you want to use a keyboard, a controller, or a steering wheel. You can only manually edit each function individually.
- It appears that you cannot freely rotate the camera around. Why? Every other driving game out there can. It's pretty much standard practice. Oh right, I forgot. That whole "common sense and basic human intelligence" bit I spoke about earlier.
- When driving, my car wanted to just veer to the right on its own, with no input from me. Try to get it back inline and it'll just repeat the process. Naturally, steering the car and trying to keep it behaving normally wasn't going to happen. So, there's something going on with this game and having issues with tracking. It seems this is a bit of a known problem on the discussion boards.

That's pretty much it for pros and cons. I haven't put much time into this game because I didn't want to continue. When a game starts up and immediately comes across as a badly put-together attempt with no logical line of thinking involved, that tells me that it is not worth my time to mess with it anymore. A user shouldn't have to trudge through a sea of bulls**t to try and get the game to their liking before they even turn the wheel. There should be common practices already in place to make the first impression a welcoming one. Even the discussion threads show that the developer doesn't really bother addressing issues. And, a few users have commented about how this is another game that is going to die out real soon. I can see why.

OverDrift Festival is what happens when you get people who don't understand what the words "organized" and "thought-out" mean. Poorly laid out menu system, nonsensical mapping selection system, and broken features make this game Early-Access, in my opinion. It's not polished at all.

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
This game is at a good point and I can't wait to see where it goes. (1/16/2023)

Pros:
-Very good price!
-Open world! Freedom to go anywhere, anytime!
-Decent car selection.
-Car model visuals are good and clean.
-Lobbies are always up and you can create your own lobby, public or friends only.
-Decent tuning.
-Good controls and response.
-Damage done to car actually affects steering and power.
-Engine swaps and exhausts change how your car sounds.
-Research tree makes sense for upgraded parts and encourages you to use the same car until you master it.

Cons:
-The research tree for the cars is kinda goofy. If I want to grind and buy an old ass mustang, I should not have to research multiple cars I don't want just to get it.
-The open world is cool and all, but it is surprisingly small. I would love to see a larger city or multiple zones.
-The world feels empty at times.
-The AI cars in the world are so dumb and just get in the way or just stop in the road at critical locations. The AI cars need to be spread out in critical locations and more densely populated in less critical locations. For example, when driving in boring straight line areas there's no cars, then when you want to hit a big corner, there's a traffic jam of 5+ cars right at the peak of the corner.
-The world is linear and feels like its just missing more interesting spots and there's no backdrop, just open ocean.
-Car cosmetic customization is boring and pointless. All body kits and aero is preset on each car and cannot be changed. I would like for my car to feel more personal.
-Tuning is good, but you can't have full control over ride height and offset etc.

It is at a good place and gets large updates at a reasonable pace. CarX Drift Racing is far superior in customization, but I think if this game keeps improving it will be my new favorite. Overall, I give this game a solid 6.5/10!

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
Game is fun, drifting is easy. It just got better with the addition of a car I really wanted and fast travel having become possible. The game is generally very stable, with just one crash on Steam Deck in my many hours of playing. 

On the topic of the Steam Deck: with low settings, game set to lowest Resolution Scale and only View Distance to high and Tire Smoke on (it's a drifting game after all) and FSR on with sharpness to 5, it plays and looks great, with either 40 fps for up to 3,5 hours of playtime or 60 fps with up to 2,5 hours of playtime. 

The car selection is big enough that you have options, but not so big that those options become meaningless. The map is big enough as well. 

I like it, certainly if you can get it at a discount. DLC is optional, all DLC cars are in game, but they have a different livery in the DLC with second tier upgrades installed.

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
So I played NFS-U 1, & 2, Carbon, World, the 1st Project Torque, & well pretty much all of the popular games made since Y2K that had drifting in them...

    I know everybody has different ideas about which games are better, but I've done more doughnuts (forward, & reverse) in empty mall parking lots, & drifted on, and offroad IRL then most gamers. This game is awesome in every way, & unless there's a lag hiccup from a bunch'a people joining at the same time, pretty much every time I crash (solo, not into other players), I feel like it was My fault, whereas had I let off the gas more, or hit E-brake, I would NOT have crashed. The same cannot be said for lots of games (I just refunded Car-X drift racing because today because the opposite is true about THAT game, it ALWAYS slides to the outside after about 2 seconds as if you're on ice, & it's NOT fun).

   My point is that this game is actually MORE like real drifting then even Need for speed games (yes even U2), more realistic in what happens when the driver does ______ in every situation. It also happens to be F-U-N to play.

     The car models are cool, You can change LOTS of things: i.e. wheels, tires, window tint/stickers, put like a thousand stickers on the car, etc. PLUS you can tune the living daylights of of them, THAT fact coupled with the options in the settings for controls, makes this game's drifting awesome.

    It is online only ATM, which IMHO should be changed in the future to include offline mode, but there's always at least one up, & ready server running at the top of the list with 0 players in it, PLUS We can all host our own servers, changing things like time of day, season, & precipitation mode.

       There's a LOT of graphic options, & it runs great on My modest gaming laptop maxed (cinematic on all settings), so it'll probably run on Your' potato! I have no idea what the music is like, I turn it off on all games to listen to My 9,000 song Spotify like list.

    I've tried a PS4-pro dualshock, & use My Power-A Xbox-One controller, which is My preference, but they both worked great (You HAVE to go into settings, & setup Your' controller's binds before it'll work)

     So all, & all this game is completely awesome, I'd change a few things if I were a Dev (body kits/mods/stock, carbon fiber paint, NPCs on the amusement park rides, & drivers in the traffic vehicles, & Our' cars, plus the ability to kick, or ban players from Our' servers, AND the ability to host with no 2 hour time limit), but it's very cool now, & My new favorite car game.

BTW this game has lying developers who cater to the whales who give them the most money... LOTS of players have been complaining about how hard it is to drive from place to place on the map because of the "ALWAYS drift as if the E-brake is locked on" (which is ridiculous also), & the devs SAID multiple times they'd fix it, THEN claimed to alter the game's handling in like 6 updates, & ALL they did was adjust the game's different camera views, it handles EXACTLY the same, & yes I've completely reinstalled it more then once. The game's fun at 1st, but ramming, trolly, toxic 3rd world punks (just like what ruins MOST games), BAD drifting physics, especially with the help options toggled either way which makes wheel players be able to accelerate TWICE as fast, AND drift faster, plus the drift ALL the time ruin this game!

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
I used to drift alot on Forza Horizon 1/3/4 and on FiveM Velocity Drift servers and this game is very easy to pick up and get sliding

It is more on the arcade side, at the time on writing you have to manually set controller keybinds and such but Controllers, Keyboards/Mouse, and Wheels all work well with the game. I personally use a controller. 
Theres no visual parts customization other then Paint/Vinals, Rims/Tires, Neon and Mufflers. There are upgrade parts and all cars come with bodykits and the such, and IMO most are done very well. 
The Livery editor is amazing, better the Forza's, better then Heat's [imo] by far. Your not applying layers to just the side or the hood, you can seemlessly [mostly] flow layers from say the hood to the windows to the doors with little editing compaired to the other games [depending on how well you pick up the controls and such]. Theres a good amount of decals as well and some of the liverys I've seen already are amazing.

All round, I recommend the game. The price I say is a bit steap, I'd say $20 or $25 would be better but its not bad for the price. Community is great too, Shout out "Drift and Friends" crew and "Lost Souls" crew.

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
Allright so... drifting is fun, upgrading and collecting cars is as well. The cars themselves are really cool looking with a few nice applicable upgrades (bot performance and optical). But there are some issues that need to be fixed. But since it's early access, I can forgive them for now. The issues (+solutions) are:

- a disconnect results in loosing your earned points (not cool)
- not that much to do besides farming currencies and delivering stuff, more single-player challenges would be nice
- cars barely steer outside of drifting making it hard to correct minor mistakes or steering in general outside of drifting
- more explanations needed: you're set free in the world without any idea what to do, how to do things etc. Just a tiny bit more hand-holding would be amazing
- no explanation why I can only have two cars in my garage (how to increase that?)
- traveling the world can be annoying, because you have to break to see a bigger map and then all roads are maze-like (navigation-functionality needed)
- oncoming traffic can be (for some) be quite annoying -> make it a option to disable collision

But that's it from me. Thus far, I am enjoying my time and can recommend this for some people wanting to experiment a bit and who love to drift.

Other than

Hope to see this game progress into something amazing.

Feels like a drift version of Forza Horizon! Drifting here is a little difficult (i'm using logitech G29), gets better with car upgrades but it's still pretty challenging!
(anyone else using a g29 and found perfect settings do share!)

Map needs major improvement, a route to destination is a must. I have to open the map every few corners to check where I need to go for the next food delivery or camera. Need an option to turn off rotating map as well so it stays in a fixed position.

Another issue is when the host quits the lobby, everyone gets kicked out without warning. Probably will be good to pass on the host to another random player in the lobby instead.

Graphics is awesome as well but it makes a ton of frame drops, some optimization will be needed.

Looking forward to next updates!

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
OK this might be long but ive been playing this game for a week an half on a controller and g29 wheel OK so these are some things that can be tweak.

1.Add full controller and wheel support plus accessories (hand brake).
2.physic for controller isn't sync well.
3.Add feature to adjust caster on fully upgraded cars. 
4.physic for tires can be seriously adjusted when on controller and gaming wheel the turning registration only register when driving super slow. 
5.adjust smoothness for head turning in cockpit camera setting.
6.add feature that physically/visually changes tire compound when upgrading tires. 
7.add feature to open hood/car doors/and trunks.
8.add feature to create custom driver for in car only.
9.add feature to save tunes.
10.the most important feature of them all add clutch with manual transmission. 
11.fix vinyl for front and rear bumper vinyl will not show.
12.add window tint vinyl.
13.add feature for upgrading transmission (example 3 gears,4 gears,5 gears) street sport pro racing transmission.
14.add feature that doubles drift points when in or hitting clipping points.
15.add feature to change cockpit features like steering wheel gauges rear view mirror shifter hand brake driver seat tow hooks basic jdm /drift things 

im pretty sure im missing some small things but i know the community will comment what they are so please add these features to the next update.

review: Don’t buy and please help me refund , regrettably I gave this game a chance spent the ridiculous 38 Pounds on it hoping it would be up to that standard but unless this goes down to 5-10 pound don’t buy it not worth it at all . The psychics are weird and clunky your either to slidey or you can’t slide at all the cars just feel like paper or massive blocks of concrete nothing feels right in this game so in all the main point of the game which is drifting feels off and weird most times just annoying to play , assetto corsa is easier to play than this game and that’s a realistic sim the other gameplay elements like the horrible grind for money is ridiculous just drift around a roundabout for hours to get money not fun or has any point just a wait timer at that point to get your cars upgraded which barely feel any different anyway the weird damage system and constantly repairing your car is just annoying and literally none has ever asked for that in a drifting game especially an arcade one . The 2 hour server uptimes is so terribly designed and frustrating if the server ends before you cash in your points you lose all your money . The only good thing about the game is maybe the map with all the different tracks on it but without fast travel this can become a chore to get around especially with the bad psychics making you constantly slide out and crash along the way . This game does nothing better than assetto corsa , forza horizon 5 or carx drift racing there’s literally no point to buy it just get one of them instead there leagues ahead of this game and even still this is basically just trying to copy paste forza but with only drift cars just get forza don’t get this . I gave the game a chance spent 5 hours on it and regret it massively PLEASE LET ME REFUND

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
I like the Game, but there still is a lot to add. I use a Controller, and after setting the Bindings up once i was good to go.
The Handling feels like a Mix of Horizon 5 and CarX, but its not as predictable too me yet, altough that might comes with more playtime. Upgrading your Car makes the Car feel different, but once upgraded there is no way to swap in a lower Tier Part. Skipping a Middle Tier Part and directly installing the High-End Part Results in the Same Performance as buying all Parts of every Tier(Money Waste!).

Money
 Money and Rep is earned by Drifting, as well a the Universal  Rep.
Money is used to buy Parts.
Rep is earned per Car seperatly to unlock higher Tier Parts for it.
Universal Rep can unlock Cars or Parts on any Car, but is harder to get.

Car List

The Car is is good, there are many Cars to add, as example there is no Mercedes at all, or very few Us-Muscle Cars.

Tuning

We need more Customisation, the only changable Things on Cars are Engine, Exhaust, Wheels and Colors+Decals.

Open Word

The Open World feels very empty, there are very few Cars around and there is nothing to do besides Some Drift Cams and Delivering Food.

Positves:
+Important Cars are already in the Game
+The Game runs well
+Lobby System and Playing online works well

Negatives:
-A lot of Cars to add
-No Bodykit Customisation
-There should be more Dyno Options like in CarX
-Empty Open World
-pricey for the amount of Content

I really hope the Developers take their time and improve the Game further.

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
A MUST-HAVE IF YOU LIKE DRIFTING!

MAP: A vibrant open-world made for drifting, includes real life track replicas like Ebisu, touge roads, jump drift tracks, industrial and leisure areas. AI traffic cars. Great variation and breakable objects everywhere, a lot of fun to drift.

ONLINE: 16 players online, private or open lobbies, change time of day and weather with 4 presets. 

PHYSICS: On controller its similar to Forza where you have to countersteer, but it's easier than Forza to learn imo, and with car upgrades it's easy to get good and tandem quickly. On wheel I haven't tried it, what I hear is its okay but needs improving and to be more realistic.

CUSTOMISATION: Paint and livery editor, lots of choice, you could make pretty much anything. Performance upgrades like weight engine tyres etc. Tuning system for springs camber toe caster etc. Custom neon underglow. Engine and exhaust sounds with lots of options. Change wheels, tyre sponsors. Change exhaust pipe. No body kits or other parts but cars come stock with a cool drift kit already.

GRAPHICS: Very good graphics at max, with lots of settings to change for FPS and your PC.

EVENTS / CONTENT: Deliveries for money. Exchange drift points for money at dropoff locations. Vehicle damage (not visual) / Car repair shops. Collectables / Easter eggs. Drift corner events. No track events or leaderboards. 

OVERALL: For 30$ its a bargain, fills a big gap for drifting games! Great online experience, map is just perfect for drifting. Good customisation. Physics are good on controller but may need improvements on wheel. A really good game as it is now (first week) and with a lot more potential if there are continuous improvements and updates!

SUGGESTIONS: Waypoints - Reset Car Button - Body Kits / More Parts - Improved Wheel Physics - Free Camera Mode - Controller Preset (no mapping needed)

7.5/10

Reviewed by Smokey Wolf:
(Drift Gaming YouTuber) I made one of the steam trailers, about 20 hours on Overdrift in the first week, and 1000s on other games like forza, carx ac and more ;)

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
Ok first of all the game is really good. Ive been using the wheel and tuned stuff etc and it feels nice. Now i want to give some feedback , hopefully devs see it. The game lacks a waypoint system , it sucks having to open the map 20 times to see if i go the right way. Seccond issue is that keyboard players cant tandem with wheel players. i tested this with a friend for like 10 hours and we found out that on keyboard when u turn, (with steering assist on or off, doesnt matter) the wheels move likes pancakes so when the car goes sideways and u want to correct something the wheels turn completely to the other side causing the car to slow down , that means a keyboard player cant catch up to a wheel player , i used a 200hp car with max psi so i slide and dont gain speed and i friend used 700 hp car with lowest psi to get more grip , he still couldnt catch up even tho i was going wide and he was going on the inside. Either make the keyboard players not lose speed when the wheels turn like pancakes or i dunno. if that doesnt get fixed then keyboard and wheel players cant tandem. Next id like to adress how there is no feedback/support page on their website. Paying 25 euros for an early access game and not having a basic waypoint system nor a feedback/support page is deffo a reg flag for me. Other issue i found is that tuning has low to no impact on the car and upgrades play a more significant part. only tuning i found works is the engine tuning and u need to lower it almost completely to see a change of speed. Also when tuning the numbers go up or down only a low amount , why is max camber 8 for example? The game  needs to get more in depth with the tuning and i also hope to see more repair stations and more drift tracks around the map cuz there are some parts that are completely empty, i know its early access so more content will be added. Also wheel support feels kinda meh. i had to bind every key for my wheel to work , so please add plug and play feature. Also u cant remap arrow keys which move the menu so now i need to use keyboard instead of wheel arrows everytime i open the menu.

 So to recap:

- Fix the speed loss for keyboard and controller players get when trying to correct the steering cuz wheels move like pancakes and cause the car to slow down so it ruins tandems.
- Better plug and play feature for controllers and wheel with more keybind options ( also please let us bind 2 buttons into 1).
- More Repair stations and maybe lower the cost a bit.
- Let us remove traffic when making a server.
- Make tuning have more of an impact than the actuall upgrades.
- Make a waypoint system so we dont have to open the map all the time.
- Make a community livery section so people can share liveries they make ( this is a bit of QOL change cuz i know the game is still early access).
- ADD VOTE TO KICK FEATURE FOR TROLLERS

These are my thoughts and i hope the devs see it. If they implement these changes and ESPECIALLY  change the loss of speed that keyboard players get from steering with the wheels moving like pancakes , i think the game will become really successfull.

name: OverDrift Festival
review: I have 2 hours played in this game and to my surprise it is not so bad, considering that it is in early access. Good handling model and the roads on the map are fun to drift on. Tuning is under developed, oversteering physics can be a little weird. It would work more graphics settings and a little more optimized. If the game looks like this when it's in early access, I can't wait to see when it will officially open. Overdrift Crew continue to develop this game, it will become a great game. <3

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
Do not buy if you plan on using a steering wheel, at least for now. Wheel support is nearly non-existent and it feels like if an arcade racer tried to make it feel realistic but over tuned all the values. However, the controller feels fine. 
*edit* 
After the most recent update with physics changes wheel drivability is much better and more fun, however still not on the level of games like carX, Asetto or Forza this is a good change, but there are still other issues, here's a list.
1. very little to do in the game 
2. navigation is annoying without waypoints or better GPS
3. cars are locked to pre-customized body kits and all tires are super stretched
4. input mapping is a chore and very limited  
5. car Ai is just annoying and ruins driving on the open road with how narrow the roads are 
6. the car damage system only acts as an annoyance towards players and serves no point except to take players money and extend the already grindy progression

name: OverDrift Festival
review: The game is very good but lacks decent steering wheel support. You can adjust the settings but even then there is an extreme learning curve. I will still be playing this game daily, and hope to see different support come in at a later date.

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
Terrible Wheel setup, switching to controller is even more dumb. They made LFB drop your rpms with throttle pinned. Car oversteers and sometimes just randomly steers itself without input. Using the clutch to correct oversteer is a joke, doesn't seem to do anything except make your rpms pinned and stay there. 

The only thing good about this game is the idea of open world drifting. If they can make the cars fun to drive without losing your mind I'll pick it up again. Until then I'm going to save my money.

name: OverDrift Festival
review: great game. it costs 24 quid and so far feel like ive got my 24 quid back. the games livery customization is an underrated part of this game,the wide veriety of decals and stickers really allows you to make your car stand out. the abundance of cars in this game with perfect bodykits and (imo) great progression method really makes the game a go to compared to other games. the general controls are the main problem currently, you cant have multiple set ups ( for example wheel and then switch to controller ) because each time you change the desired device, you have to input each button again. the oversteer problem is clear to see and for current wheel support, not tip top. however i bought this game because i can see it future potential. i hope the devs spend hard time and love into this game, if you have a spare £24 lying around, definitely give it a go on both wheel and controller if accessable.

name: OverDrift Festival
review: This game is SUPER fun and pretty good for being available for barely over 24 hours. The only things I can recommend are: Having a waypoint functionality and more intuitive tuning. It's kind of annoying having to pause the game every few seconds to see where you're trying to go, and realize you missed a turn a while ago and have to double-back. I think the tuning is okay, its just not very obvious what to do. I don't think you should copy it exactly...but a tuning system similar to Forza Horizon would be spectacular.

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
So im 10 hours in - what do I think?

While im no drifting expert, I've always loved to drift in Forza MS/Horizon, NFS games etc and have played a lot of Car X Drift Online.

Initially when I started playing, I was considering getting a refund because I was spinning out on every corner with the starter car. Didnt matter how I tuned it, it always span out. Then I went and tried some other cars, put some upgrades into them and while I wasnt getting stupid angles, I was making points/money/etc to spend on further upgrades.

While there isnt much "to do" in the game in terms of comps/events, I have been having a lot of fun just cruising from track to track, doing the odd car meet with other players and doing the odd bit of touge.

Loads of cars but not a huge amount of customisation outside of changing your muffler and decals so would love to see further custom options.

Personally - worth the money IMO

name: OverDrift Festival
review: There is a lot of potential in this game, but for right now, that potential is all it has. I ONLY RECOMMEND BUYING THIS CURRENTLY IF YOU ARE READY TO PLAY A BARE-BONES AND VERY UNFINISHED GAME. This game has a lot of development it still needs to go under, and I mean a lot. However, I can already tell it will shape up to be a great game.  So in conclusion, buy this if you are prepared to see the potential of and play a very unfinished game,

name: OverDrift Festival
review: This game definitely have massive potential to be one of the top drift games. Once all the bugs get worked out, i can definitely see this being in the same league as Car X Drifting. The game really needs better controller support for sure and a lot more to do. It is really fun to drive around and drift in the world, it feels alive unlike other drift games but there are hardly any activities at the moment. Also i feel like having someone in the car would bring a touch of realism to the game, the car being empty is just creepy to me lol and it feels weird. I bought the game day one and its already really fun. Looking forward to seeing how this game progresses and its comeuppance in the drifting game world. At this stage, I'd give it a 8/10 due to lack of controller support.

name: OverDrift Festival
review:
As long as you are coming into this with the understanding that it is an early build and that it was made public relatively recently, it is a good thing to waste time on! Cars are incredibly nervous though, so I HIGHLY recommend softening up the suspension, loosening up the diff and so on. Good start thus far, will be interested to see where this goes!

Don't expect it to be CarX though, you can feel the difference in physics, and there's not a lot of visual customization (though changing how the exhaust sounds when bouncing off the limiter is a neat touch!)
